questions.q: Of which island kingdom was Ravana the king?
questions.explanation: Ravana was the rakshasa emperor who ruled the golden kingdom of Lanka.

questions.q: How many heads is Ravana traditionally depicted as having?
questions.explanation: Ravana is famously known as the ten-headed king, the Dashagriva.

questions.q: Whose wife did Ravana abduct, setting the war in motion?
questions.explanation: Ravana abducted Sita, the wife of Rama, which brought Rama's wrath upon him.

questions.q: Which kind of being was Ravana?
questions.explanation: Ravana was the mighty emperor of the rakshasas, a race of demons.

questions.q: Which god was Ravana a devoted worshipper of?
questions.explanation: Ravana was a learned and ardent devotee of Lord Shiva.

questions.q: Who ultimately killed Ravana in battle?
questions.explanation: Rama slew Ravana on the battlefield of Lanka, ending his tyranny.

questions.q: What is Ravana's title that means 'ten-faced one'?
questions.explanation: Ravana is called Dashamukha (or Dashagriva), meaning the ten-faced or ten-necked one.

questions.q: Who was Ravana's chief queen?
questions.explanation: Mandodari was Ravana's principal queen and a woman renowned for her virtue.

questions.q: Who was Ravana's loyal brother who fought alongside him in the war?
questions.explanation: Kumbhakarna, the giant brother of Ravana, fought and died for him in the war.

questions.q: In Hindu tradition, Ravana's defeat is celebrated during which festival?
questions.explanation: Dussehra (Vijayadashami) celebrates Rama's victory over Ravana, marked by burning his effigy.

questions.q: Who was the father of Ravana?
questions.explanation: Ravana was the son of the sage Vishrava and the rakshasi Kaikesi.

questions.q: Who was Ravana's righteous brother who defected to join Rama?
questions.explanation: Vibhishana, the virtuous brother, left Ravana and allied with Rama after being insulted.

questions.q: What was the name of Ravana's powerful son who defeated Lakshmana before dying?
questions.explanation: Indrajit (Meghnad), Ravana's son, was a mighty warrior who fell to Lakshmana in battle.

questions.q: Ravana's sister, whose disfigurement helped provoke the conflict, was named what?
questions.explanation: Surpanakha was Ravana's sister; her mutilation by Lakshmana spurred Ravana toward revenge.

questions.q: From whom did Ravana usurp Lanka and the flying chariot Pushpaka?
questions.explanation: Ravana seized Lanka and the Pushpaka Vimana from his half-brother Kubera, the god of wealth.

questions.q: What boon did Ravana win from Brahma through severe penance?
questions.explanation: Ravana gained invulnerability from gods, demons, and other celestial beings, but not from humans.

questions.q: Why did Ravana not seek protection from humans in his boon from Brahma?
options: He despised humans as too weak to threaten him
questions.explanation: Ravana disdained mortals as insignificant, an oversight that allowed the human Rama to slay him.

questions.q: Disguised as what did Ravana approach to abduct Sita?
questions.explanation: Ravana came to Sita's hermitage disguised as a sannyasi (mendicant) before carrying her off.

questions.q: Which bird tried to stop Ravana as he carried Sita away and was mortally wounded?
questions.explanation: The noble vulture Jatayu fought Ravana to save Sita and was fatally wounded in the attempt.

questions.q: In which grove of Lanka did Ravana hold Sita captive?
questions.explanation: Sita was kept prisoner in the Ashoka Vatika (Ashoka grove) within Ravana's kingdom.

questions.q: What was the name of Ravana's grandfather on his mother's side, a rakshasa lord?
questions.explanation: Sumali was the rakshasa lord whose daughter Kaikesi was Ravana's mother.

questions.q: Ravana's ten heads are traditionally said to symbolize his mastery of what?
options: The six shastras and four Vedas
questions.explanation: His ten heads signify command of the six shastras and four Vedas, vast learning marred by pride.

questions.q: What was the original name of Ravana before he earned the name 'Ravana'?
questions.explanation: He was first called Dashagriva; the name Ravana ('one who makes others roar') came after lifting Mount Kailasa.

questions.q: According to tradition, how did Ravana earn the very name 'Ravana' from Shiva?
options: By attempting to lift and shake Mount Kailasa
By winning a duel with Indra
questions.explanation: When he tried to uproot Kailasa, Shiva pinned him; his cries earned him the name Ravana, 'the roarer'.

questions.q: The musical instrument Ravana is said to have created and played in Shiva's honor is the what?
questions.explanation: Ravana is credited with crafting the bowed instrument known as the Ravanahatha.

questions.q: In a former life, Ravana was said to be one of which pair of cursed gatekeepers of Vishnu's abode?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds Ravana was Jaya (with Kumbhakarna as Vijaya), a doorkeeper of Vaikuntha cursed to be born a demon.

questions.q: Which celestial weapon-curse limited Ravana from forcing himself upon Sita?
options: A curse from Nalakubara for assaulting Rambha
questions.explanation: Nalakubara cursed Ravana that his head would burst if he ever forced himself on an unwilling woman.

questions.q: On the eve of his death, whom did Rama send to learn statecraft and wisdom from the dying Ravana?
questions.explanation: In tradition, Rama sent Lakshmana to the fallen Ravana to receive his counsel on kingship and life.

questions.q: Where in Ravana's body did Rama strike the fatal arrow to finally kill him?
questions.explanation: Rama's Brahmastra struck Ravana's navel, said to hold the nectar that sustained his life.

questions.q: Which conqueror-king is Ravana famously recorded as having once defeated and imprisoned, the ruler of the celestials?
questions.explanation: Ravana's son captured Indra, king of the gods, earning the son the title Indrajit ('conqueror of Indra').
